---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
---

```{=html}
<p style="margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">
  <a href="assets/cv.pdf" class="btn btn-outline-dark btn-sm" download>
    <i class="bi bi-download"></i> Download PDF
  </a>
</p>
```

## Eli Cheam Shapiro, MRes

elicheamshapiro@gmail.com | [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/eli-cheam-shapiro) | Citizenship: United States

---

## Education

**Institut d'Études Politiques de Paris (Sciences Po)** · Paris, France
*Master of Research in Economics* · August 2023 – June 2025
GPA: 4.0/4.0 (Converted by Institution)

**Bard College** · Annandale-on-Hudson, United States
*Bachelor of Arts in Economics* · August 2019 – May 2023
GPA: 3.8/4.0
Awards: Levy Economics Institute Prize (2023); OSUN Certificate in Public Policy and Economic Analysis (2023); Amicus Foundation Scholarship (2021)

---

## Skills & Coursework

**Technical Skills:** R; STATA; Python; Julia; LaTeX; Salesforce; Microsoft Office; Optical Character Recognition; Census Matching; Panel and Cross-sectional Econometrics; DSGE Modeling; Randomized Control Trials

**Languages:** English (Native); French (B1); Khmer/Cambodian (Basic Work Proficiency)

**Graduate Coursework:** Advanced Macroeconomics I, II, III; Advanced Microeconomics I, II; Advanced Econometrics I, II, III; Development Economics; Public Economics; Economic History; Computational Economics; Political Economy; International Economics

---

## Professional Experience

**Penn Wharton Budget Model** · Philadelphia, United States
*Analyst (Full-Time)* · June 2026 – Present

**Department of Economics, Trinity College Dublin** · Dublin, Ireland
*Predoctoral Research Assistant (Full-Time)* · September 2025 – June 2026

- Consulted on data processing and econometric methodology for research papers
- Developed OCR (Python), census matching and cleaning (STATA) models to generate a large database of Russian Empire administrative and business surveys
- Built webscraping models (Python) to construct databases of 100,000 foreigners in pre-revolution Russia and to extract tables and graphs from House Commons Papers
- Developed VLM- and LLM-based OCR pipelines to convert historical PDFs into usable data
- Tested and improved OCR models on the 1845–1914 Thom's Street Directory reports from an HPC

**The Economic Journal, Royal Economic Society** · Paris, France
*Replicator (Part-Time)* · August 2024 – June 2025

- Replicated journal paper submissions under the supervision of Dr. Florian Oswald
- Identified errors in submission code and provided recommendations

**Kiel Institute for the World Economy** · Remote
*Research Intern (Full-Time)* · July 2024 – September 2024

- Supported research by Dr. Christoph Trebesch and Dr. Johannes Marzian on militarization, public finance, and geopolitics; contributed to a working paper
- Collected, cleaned, and analyzed historical public finance data for 20+ advanced economies (1870–2020) in R and STATA
- Produced 200+ visualizations for exploratory analysis

**Ceres, Inc.** · Remote
*Financial Regulation Intern (Full-Time)* · June 2023 – August 2023

- Produced reports on international, federal, and state financial regulation policies
- Wrote an 11-page internal report on improving the Federal Reserve's climate-risk scenario analysis

**Bard College Department of Economics** · Annandale, United States
*Economics Tutor (Part-Time)* · March 2022 – May 2023

**Mekong Strategic Group** · Phnom Penh, Cambodia
*Analytics Intern (Full-Time)* · June 2022 – August 2022

**OSUN – Economic Democracy Initiative** · Annandale, United States
*Student Fellow (Full-Time)* · June 2021 – August 2021

**Office of Illinois State Senator Heather Steans** · Chicago, United States
*Summer Intern (Full-Time)* · June 2020 – August 2020

---

## Leadership & Activities

- New York Southeast Asia Society, Member · 2021 – Present
- Young Scholars Initiative, Student Member · 2019 – Present
- Bard College Economics Club, President · 2020 – 2022
